title: Pancreator workflow stage
description: One stage file at library/workflows/<slug>/stages/<stage-slug>.json. A stage is one unit of delegated work with a persona, a task prompt, a workspace mutation policy, a gate, a declared output shape, criteria, and transitions.

slug.description: Stage identifier, unique within the workflow. Must match the file name and appear in the workflow index stages[].

title.description: Human-readable stage name shown on invocation cards and records.

persona.description: Owner of the stage. Resolves to library/personas/<persona>.md and, for delegated work, to the matching .cursor/agents/<persona>.md subagent.

executor.description: Execution mode. agent delegates to a worker persona. harness runs deterministic runtime logic without delegation.

prompt_path.description: Repository-relative path to the stage task brief. Its contents become the Task section of the invocation card.

workspace_policy.description: Mutation boundary enforced by the harness via workspace fingerprints. source_allowed: may modify product source. release_metadata_only: in self-development ship stages, may modify only release metadata and durable version-bearing documentation; in embedded installations it behaves as read_only. runtime_only: may write only under runtime/ (planning, intake). read_only: may not change tracked content. Any policy other than source_allowed adds the deterministic criterion scope.no_unapproved_changes.

gate.description: What decides advancement after a successful output. operator: pause for explicit operator approval (intake, ship). supervisor: pause for independent supervisor judgment of the judgment criteria (plan). stage_verdict: the worker's own verdict drives the transition (review, test). next_stage: advance directly to the success transition (implement).

context.description: Stage-scoped context projection. Required inputs are read before work; conditional references are opened only on their declared trigger; omitted history remains discoverable through a generated context manifest.

request.description: Whether the original operator request is a required input, a fallback reference, or omitted from the stage card.

required_stage_outputs.description: Effective stage outputs the worker must read. Missing selectors are surfaced explicitly on the invocation card.

conditional_stage_outputs.description: Stage outputs that remain available but should be read only when required inputs do not resolve the task or unresolved remediation evidence requires them.

prior_attempts.description: Maximum prior attempts of the current stage to include as required remediation context.

operator_feedback.description: Maximum most-recent operator feedback artifacts targeting this stage to include as required inputs.

include_active_waivers.description: Include only waivers still bound to the latest stage attempt and current workspace fingerprint.

include_workspace_ratifications.description: Include the latest operator workspace ratification matching the current fingerprint.

include_latest_ledger_validation.description: Include the latest harness-owned workspace-change validation result. The field name is retained for persisted-state compatibility.

required_data.description: Shape contract for the output's data object, as a map of dotted paths to expected JSON types. The harness rejects an output whose data does not match. Type is one of: object, array, string, number, boolean.

criteria.description: Checkable claims for this stage. See governance/criteria/index.md for naming and families.

type.description: judgment: evaluated by a reasoning agent. shell: harness reruns command. state: harness checks run state and fingerprints.

hard.description: If true, failure or not_applicable blocks the stage. Defaults to false.

statement.description: The claim, phrased so that pass is unambiguous.

command.description: Required for type=shell. The exact command the harness reruns; never assembled from agent-controlled values.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout for a shell criterion. Defaults to 120000.

transitions.description: Outcome routing. Targets are either another stage slug or a terminal status (succeeded, failed, canceled, paused).

success.description: Target after a successful, gated outcome.

failure.description: Remediation target after a failed outcome (often the implement stage).

blocked.description: Target when the worker reports a blocker; usually 'paused'.

selection.description: latest selects the newest attempt regardless of outcome; latest_success selects the newest successful attempt.
